title: Responsive CSS, Reflow, Progressive Enhancement, and Performance Budgets · Behavior Case: Performance Evidence
summary: Generated pages assume one viewport, content clips at zoom, navigation depends on JavaScript, fonts block text, images shift layout, and a fast home page hides slow long-form routes. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the performance evidence increment in responsive progressively enhanced performance budget. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Measure cold and warm representative routes across mobile, tablet, desktop, slow networks, long content, missing cache, and disabled JavaScript.
Budget HTML, CSS, JavaScript, fonts, images, requests, layout shifts, and critical rendering work per representative template and route.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-responsive-performance-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-responsive-performance-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_responsive_performance_5(viewports: int = 3, reflow_passes: int = 3, page_kib: int = 180, page_budget_kib: int = 220, layout_shifts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if viewports < 3 or reflow_passes < 0:
        return False, reflow_passes, "viewport-matrix"
    if reflow_passes != viewports:
        return False, reflow_passes, "reflow-failure"
    if page_kib < 0 or page_kib > page_budget_kib:
        return False, page_kib, "resource-budget"
    if layout_shifts > 0:
        return False, layout_shifts, "layout-instability"
    assert reflow_passes == viewports
    return True, page_kib, "responsive-budget"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-performance-evidence-l6d0l6
# Competency: ssg-performance-evidence.
# Case 6305: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_performance_evidence_l6d0l6(viewports: int = 3, reflow_passes: int = 3, page_kib: int = 180, page_budget_kib: int = 220, layout_shifts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_l6d0l6 = "l6d0l6-6l0d6l-6305"
    assert evidence_variant_l6d0l6
    evidence_axes_l6d0l6 = (
        "readerl6d0l6",
        "source6305",
        "parse6l0d6l",
        "routel6d0l66305",
        "artifact6305l6d0l6",
        "recovery6l0d6l6305",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_l6d0l6)) == 6
    if viewports < 3 or reflow_passes < 0:
        return False, reflow_passes, "viewport-matrix"
    if reflow_passes != viewports:
        return False, reflow_passes, "reflow-failure"
    if page_kib < 0 or page_kib > page_budget_kib:
        return False, page_kib, "resource-budget"
    if layout_shifts > 0:
        return False, layout_shifts, "layout-instability"
    assert reflow_passes == viewports
    return True, page_kib, "responsive-budget"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Performance Evidence model · Theory Performance Eviden…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Performance Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 5822 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure cold and warm representative routes across mobile, tablet, desktop, slow networks, long content, missing cache, and disabled JavaScript.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Performance Evidence model · Theory Performance Eviden….
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Performance Evidence model · Theory Performance Eviden…, keep Performance Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Measure cold and warm representative routes across mobile, tablet, desktop, slow networks, long content, missing cache, and disabled JavaScript.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Performance Evidence model · Theory Performance Eviden…, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Performance Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Performance Evidence model · Theory Performance Eviden…, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 5822 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure cold and warm representative routes across mobile, tablet, desktop, slow networks, long content, missing cache, and disabled JavaScript.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Performance Evidence model · Theory Performance Eviden…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A local desktop load time predicts field performance.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-performance-evidence-l6d0l6.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-performance-evidence-l6d0l6(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_performance_evidence_l6d0l6\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?viewports < 3 or reflow_passes < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reflow_passes != viewports)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?page_kib < 0 or page_kib > page_budget_kib)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?layout_shifts > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
